reasoning: The 1-year expected inflation series has fluctuated between roughly 2.18 and 2.89 over the past year, with an average around 2.64. The most recent reading of 2.704 is slightly above the 12-month mean, making mean reversion slightly more favored. Additionally, the recent momentum of the series has been downward over the last three monthly observations. Although a statistical trend estimator places the probability of an increase at 0.58 due to an overall slight upward drift year-over-year, the recent bearish momentum and the value's position above the mean suggest an increase in the short term is slightly less than 50/50. Therefore, I estimate the probability of an increase by December 2025 at 0.47. Following the principle that uncertainty increases with longer forecast horizons (which pulls the estimate toward the uninformed prior of 0.5), I forecast a probability of 0.49 for the February 2026 reading.
